scenario: Catalyst SaaS runs the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot Starter to drive 1500 jobs per minute. The platform team is debugging timeout-related warnings in worker logs and needs to confirm the documented default value of camunda.client.request-timeout so the team can decide whether to extend it per command or only in specific code paths.
question: What is the default value of camunda.client.request-timeout in the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot Starter properties reference?

options.text: PT10S, the default request timeout used by the Camunda client when a specific command does not override it on a per-call basis
PT5S, the default request timeout used by the Camunda client when a specific command does not override it on a per-call basis
PT30S, the default request timeout used by the Camunda client when a specific command does not override it on a per-call basis
PT60S, the default request timeout used by the Camunda client when a specific command does not override it on a per-call basis

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client section lists camunda.client.request-timeout with a default value of PT10S, defined as the request timeout to use when not overridden by a specific command.

b.text: Incorrect. PT5S is the documented default for the OAuth credentials provider connect-timeout and read-timeout under camunda.client.auth, not the client request timeout.

c.text: Incorrect. The properties reference does not list PT30S as the default for camunda.client.request-timeout; the documented default is PT10S.

d.text: Incorrect. PT60S is not the documented default for camunda.client.request-timeout; the reference shows PT10S.

explanation: The Spring Boot Starter properties reference sets camunda.client.request-timeout to a default of PT10S. That value applies whenever a specific command does not pass its own request timeout, so commands that need a longer or shorter timeout must override it explicitly.
